title: Centaurea nitiida
intro: Centaurea nitiida, also known as mountain centaury, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea nitiida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Mountain centaury is traditionally used in herbal medicine to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Centaurea nitiida is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Centaurea, commonly known as knapweed or cornflower. This species is a member of the tribe Cardueae, which comprises many thistle and knapweed species. The classification of Centaurea nitiida is further detailed by its botanical name, which includes the rank of species. As a species within the family Asteraceae, Centaurea nitiida shares many characteristics with other plants in this family, such as daisies and sunflowers.
